data.title: Benjamin Cowen Reflects on Academic Influence in Crypto Commentary
data.description: Benjamin Cowen shared insights into how his academic background in graduate school and laboratory research has influenced his disciplined approach to crypto commentary. According to NS3.AI, Cowen spent the years from 2013 to 2018 studying radiation damage, which helped him develop a work ethic that he now applies to his platform, IntoTheCryptoverse, maintaining a non-promotional style.

data.title: U.S. Oil Tankers Head to Ports Amid Global Energy Crisis
data.description:
The White House has announced that 103 empty oil tankers are en route to U.S. ports to load American crude oil. According to BlockBeats, this move is part of the U.S. strategy to address the global oil and gas crisis, leveraging its record energy production to provide essential support worldwide.

The Trump administration highlighted that among these vessels, 54 are Very Large Crude Carriers (VLCCs), each capable of transporting approximately 2 million barrels of crude oil. Data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration indicates that the United States is the world's largest crude oil producer, with a daily production rate of 13 million barrels before the outbreak of war.

The U.S. exports around 11 million barrels of oil daily while importing 8 million barrels, positioning the country as a net exporter of oil.

data.title: Commercial Shipping Traffic Increases in Strait of Hormuz
data.description:
Over the past 24 hours, more than 20 commercial vessels have traversed the Strait of Hormuz, according to a report by the Wall Street Journal. According to BlockBeats, while the volume of commercial shipping remains a fraction of pre-war levels, there has been an improvement in vessel traffic through this critical waterway.

One U.S. official noted that the ships passing through the strait included cargo ships, container vessels, and oil tankers entering and exiting the Persian Gulf. To mitigate the risk of Iranian attacks, some ships have turned off their transponders during navigation.

During the conflict, the threat of Iranian attacks and mines deterred most vessels from crossing this narrow passage. Currently, the United States is enforcing a blockade on Iranian ports. Ships not heading to Iranian ports are not subject to the blockade and are allowed to pass freely.

data.title: STOCKS | Nasdaq Leads U.S. Stock Market Gains with 1.96% Increase
data.description: U.S. stocks experienced an upward trend today, with the Nasdaq leading the charge by gaining 1.96%. According to NS3.AI, the S&P 500 also saw an increase of 1.18%, while the Dow Jones added 0.66%. These gains reflect a positive sentiment in the market, contributing to a strong performance across major indices.

data.title: ECB President Lagarde Warns of Economic Strain Due to Iran Conflict
data.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde has issued a warning regarding the impact of the ongoing conflict in Iran on the global economy. Bloomberg posted on X that Lagarde emphasized the strain this situation is placing on economic stability worldwide. The conflict has led to increased uncertainty in financial markets, affecting investor confidence and economic growth projections. Lagarde's comments highlight the need for vigilance and strategic planning to mitigate potential economic disruptions. The ECB continues to monitor the situation closely, assessing its implications for monetary policy and economic forecasts.

data.title: U.S. API Distillate Stocks Fall More Than Expected
data.description: The American Petroleum Institute (API) reported a significant decrease in U.S. distillate inventories for the week ending April 10. According to Jin10, the stocks fell by 3.363 million barrels, surpassing the anticipated decline of 2.5 million barrels. The previous week's data showed a decrease of 599,000 barrels. This larger-than-expected drop in distillate stocks may impact market dynamics and energy prices.

data.title: U.S. API Crude Oil Inventory Surges by 6.101 Million Barrels
data.description: The American Petroleum Institute (API) reported a significant increase in U.S. crude oil inventories for the week ending April 10. According to Jin10, the crude oil stockpiles rose by 6.101 million barrels, surpassing the expected decrease of 1.266 million barrels. The previous week's inventory level was recorded at 3.719 million barrels. This unexpected rise in crude oil inventories could have implications for the oil market, potentially affecting prices and supply dynamics.

data.title: DOJ Opens Compensation Claims for OneCoin Fraud Victims
data.description: The U.S. Department of Justice has initiated a compensation claims process for victims of the $4 billion OneCoin fraud. According to NS3.AI, investors who purchased OneCoin between 2014 and 2019 are eligible to file claims online until June 30. Attorney Jonathan Levy has expressed concerns that the remission process places the burden of proof on claimants, potentially leaving many small investors without reimbursement.

data.title: SEC Approves Removal of Pattern Day Trader Rule, Easing Entry for Retail Traders
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has approved the elimination of the Pattern Day Trader rule, which previously required traders to maintain a minimum account balance of $25,000 for day trading activities. According to NS3.AI, this regulatory change is anticipated to lower the entry barrier for smaller retail traders, potentially increasing their participation in the market. However, the implementation of this change by brokerages is expected to take place between mid-2026 and 2028.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI Introduces New AI Model for Software Security
data.description: OpenAI has unveiled a new AI model designed to enhance the detection of software security vulnerabilities. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this development comes shortly after Anthropic's announcement of its AI tool, Mythos. The introduction of OpenAI's model marks a significant step in the ongoing competition among AI companies to improve cybersecurity measures. The model is currently available to a select group of users, aiming to provide advanced capabilities in identifying potential security threats in software systems. This move underscores the increasing focus on leveraging AI technology to bolster cybersecurity defenses.

data.title: India Faces Graduate Oversupply Amid Job Market Challenges
data.description: India is experiencing an oversupply of graduates, leading to challenges in the job market. Bloomberg posted on X that the country needs to create more manufacturing jobs and shift towards vocational training to address this surplus. The current educational system produces a large number of graduates, but the job market is unable to absorb them effectively. This situation calls for a strategic pivot to vocational training, which can equip individuals with practical skills suited for various industries. By focusing on manufacturing and vocational education, India can better align its workforce with market demands, potentially reducing unemployment and enhancing economic growth.

data.title: Tim Draper Reiterates Bitcoin's $250,000 Target Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description: Tim Draper has once again predicted that Bitcoin will reach $250,000 within the next 18 months. According to NS3.AI, Draper cites inflationary pressures and a weakening U.S. dollar as key factors supporting this forecast. This is not the first time Draper has made this prediction, maintaining his optimistic outlook for Bitcoin's future value.

data.title: Russia Warns of Potential U.S.-Israel Military Action Against Iran
data.description: Russian media reported on the 14th, citing the Russian Security Council, that the United States and Israel might be preparing for a ground military operation against Iran under the guise of peace talks, as the Pentagon continues to increase its military presence in the Middle East. According to Jin10, reports from Russia's RIA Novosti and TASS indicate that the Russian Security Council has warned of the possibility of intensified hostilities within two weeks if U.S.-Iran negotiations do not achieve their intended outcomes. Additionally, it was noted that Iran still possesses a significant arsenal of weapons. The report highlighted that the Iranian government maintains control over the domestic situation, contrary to the unrest and internal turmoil that Washington and Tel Aviv might have anticipated. Iranian social and political groups have shown increased unity around the central government in response to U.S. and Israeli interference.

data.title: Argentina's Inflation Surges in March Amid Rising Fuel and Education Costs
data.description: Argentina experienced a significant increase in inflation in March, driven by sharp rises in fuel prices and education costs. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of the ongoing Iran war on fuel prices, which contributed to the inflationary pressures. Additionally, annual adjustments in tuition fees further exacerbated the situation, leading to a notable surge in education expenses. The unexpected rise in inflation underscores the challenges faced by the Argentine economy as it navigates external geopolitical tensions and domestic economic adjustments.

data.title: Kenya Fuel Prices Surge Amid Global Tensions
data.description: Kenya's fuel prices have reached their highest level in nearly three years, driven by the ongoing geopolitical tensions involving Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the economic ripple effects of the conflict, which have significantly impacted global oil markets. The increase in pump prices is a direct consequence of the instability in the region, affecting economies worldwide. As the situation continues to evolve, consumers in Kenya and other affected regions are facing the financial strain of rising energy costs.

data.title: Iran Begins Restoring Limited Internet Access Amid Economic Concerns
data.description: Iran has initiated the process of restoring limited internet connectivity to certain businesses and individuals. Bloomberg posted on X, indicating that this move is seen as an effort to alleviate the economic impact caused by the nationwide internet shutdown. The restoration comes after widespread disruptions that affected various sectors, highlighting the government's attempt to balance control with economic stability.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic's Tokenized Shares Suggest $851 Billion Market Cap on Jupiter
data.description: Tokenized pre-IPO shares of Anthropic on the Jupiter platform indicate a market capitalization of $851 billion, significantly exceeding the company's $380 billion post-money valuation from its February 2026 Series G funding round. According to NS3.AI, these synthetic tokens were introduced through PreStocks on the Solana-based decentralized exchange aggregator. The token price surged from approximately $122 in October 2025 to around $900 by April 14, 2026.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 74,000 USDT with a Narrowed 2.25% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 14, 2026, 18:47 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 74,000 USDT and is now trading at 73,990.078125 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 2.25% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Jackrong Unveils Gemopus Models Based on Google's Gemma 4
data.description: Jackrong has introduced Gemopus, a series of Claude Opus-style fine-tuned models built on Google's Gemma 4. According to NS3.AI, these models come in two variants: 26B and 4B, tailored for local use. The larger model utilizes approximately 4 billion parameters during inference, while the smaller E4B variant is optimized for devices like the iPhone 17 Pro Max and MacBook Air.

data.title: CoW Swap Suspends Services Following DNS Hijacking Incident
data.description: CoW Swap temporarily suspended its services after identifying a DNS hijacking incident on its website at 14:54 UTC on Tuesday. According to NS3.AI, the team reported that while the backend and APIs were not directly compromised, they were paused as a precautionary measure while the issue is being addressed.
